assertions.text: Vehicle was traveling east at about 85 km/h prior to drifting off the road.
Vehicle drifted off the roadway (left or right direction unspecified).
Driver applied the brakes well before leaving the roadway.
Braking occurred prior to the vehicle leaving the roadway.
The front of the car struck a bridge abutment.
There was at least one impact (the front striking the bridge abutment).
The front of the car striking the bridge abutment implies a frontal collision; no claim about other damage severity beyond that impact was made.

verdicts.evidence: The claim states [Vehicle was traveling east at about 85 km/h prior to drifting off the road.](T2) but the vehicle speed samples show values rising from [77.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s to 87.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2) and the pre-impact speed range is [77.0 km/h–90.0 km/h](T3); the specific heading (east) is not present in the telemetry, so the asserted heading is unsupported and the precise stated speed 85 km/h is within the recorded range but the heading is contradicted by absence — overall the exact claim (east at ~85 km/h) is contradicted by missing heading data and the record shows speeds around the claimed magnitude.

The telemetry does not provide a road-boundary or lane-departure channel. Steering shows a large change with peak to [−78.0 deg at t=0.0 s](T3) and increased steering beginning around [t=-1.0 s where steering is −28.0 deg](T2), but that only indicates steering input; whether the vehicle left the roadway (direction left/right) is not recorded and thus unverifiable.

The claim says [Driver applied the brakes well before leaving the roadway.](T2) but the brake channel samples show the brake was off for the entire pre-crash window with fraction on [0.0 and no transitions](T3) and raw samples all [0.0 from t=-5.0 to t=0.0](T2), so telemetry contradicts that braking was applied before the event.

The sequence claim that [Braking occurred prior to the vehicle leaving the roadway.](T2) is contradicted because the brake channel shows [no brake ON samples across t=-5.0 to t=0.0](T2) and compute stats report [fraction_on=0.0 with no transitions](T3), so there is no evidence braking occurred prior to the crash.

The claim that [The front of the car struck a bridge abutment.](T1) describes impact location; telemetry shows a large longitudinal delta-V with final longitudinal delta-V [−87.0 km/h at t=0.09 s](T3) indicating a strong deceleration consistent with a frontal impact, but device-frame axes and yaw/heading alignment are undocumented and there is no map/location or object-type channel, so the statement that it was a bridge abutment and that the front struck it cannot be fully confirmed from sensors alone and is thus unverifiable.

The claim says [There was at least one impact (the front striking the bridge abutment).](T1) and the EDR summary records [num_events_recorded=1](T1) and the crash pulse shows cumulative longitudinal delta-V peaking at [−87.0 km/h at t=90 ms](T1,T3), which supports that at least one significant impact occurred.

The claim that [The front of the car striking the bridge abutment implies a frontal collision; no claim about other damage severity beyond that impact was made.](T3) is supported in the sense that the crash pulse shows a dominant longitudinal delta-V of [−87.0 km/h](T3) much larger than lateral delta-V [−11.0 km/h peak](T1), consistent with a primarily frontal collision.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a single strong crash pulse dominated by longitudinal delta-V [−87.0 km/h](T3), no pre-impact braking [fraction_on=0.0](T3), and speeds in the [77.0–90.0 km/h range pre-impact](T2,T3). However, heading, lane departure, and object type (bridge abutment) are not recorded, yielding uncertainty for location/orientation claims.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
